market_summary: REGIME: RISK-OFF CONFIRMED — SPY close=631.97, 1m=-7.62%, 3m=-8.20%, below BOTH MA50 and MA200 (658.15); QQQ 1m=-8.07%, 3m=-10.52%, below both MAs; IWM 1m=-8.18%, also below MA200 (241.13) — broad market deterioration accelerating into April 2 'Liberation Day' tariff announcement. TLT mom=-0.69%, below MA50/MA200, no flight-to-safety bond bid — stagflation/rates-pressure backdrop fully entrenched. BTC-USD mom=-23.65%, 3m=-24.44%, dd63=-29.69% — crypto fully risk-off, crypto sleeve correctly at zero. GLD above MA200 (377.17) with buffer +9.9% ($414.58 vs $377.17) but 1m=-14.30%, dd63=-19.21% — precious metals correction persists. Regime classification: RISK-OFF/DEFENSIVE, no upgrade warranted. April 2 reciprocal tariff event is the dominant near-term binary tail risk with potential for violent gap moves in TSM, KLAC, and tech-adjacent names.

LEADERSHIP: XOM retains #1 rank — mom=+39.09%, 3m=+44.92%, 1m=+12.44%, above BOTH MA50 ($149.41) and MA200 ($121.62), dd63=-5.48%, vol20=21.48%. Best risk-adjusted momentum in the entire candidate universe by wide margin. CVX #2 — mom=+30.28%, 3m=+41.83%, 1m=+12.82%, above BOTH MAs, vol20=19.54% (lowest in portfolio), dd63=-5.28% — cleanest defensive ballast. KLAC #3 — mom=+32.05%, 3m=+8.19%, 1m=-9.31%, above MA200 ($1,163) but below MA50 ($1,476), dd63=-22.41%, vol20=53.95% — elevated vol and 1m drag but 12m=+99.01% structural thesis and EPS ramp $9.14→$12.10 intact. TSM mom=+24.10%, 3m=+4.80%, 1m=-15.27%, above MA200 ($287.03) but well below MA50 ($347.06), dd63=-18.14% — tariff drag accelerating into April 2, pre-committed monitoring active. LMT mom=+19.86%, 3m=+24.57%, 1m=-8.56%, above MA200 ($505.98) only, EPS Q1=$6.72→Q4=$8.45 — defense rearmament thesis intact. GOOGL mom=+13.83%, 3m=-12.70%, 1m=-12.21%, above MA200 ($262.92) only — persistent negative 3m momentum now the weakest equity thesis. GLD mom=+12.95%, 3m=-0.52%, above MA200 — structural inflation hedge holds. SLV mom=+29.65% composite but 1m=-25.26%, 3m=-10.69%, dd63=-42.45%, vol20=58.89% — technically most damaged holding, 2% floor weight.

GO/NO-GO: NON-REBALANCE DAY — Rebalance due = false. Days since last rebalance checkpoint = 6 (minimum cadence = 7 days; next eligible rebalance window ~2026-04-01). Mandatory do-nothing per cadence policy. Portfolio weighted avg momentum ~+27% vs SPY mom=-1.77% = +29pp alpha spread maintained despite broad market deterioration. No emergency kill-switch breaches: GLD=$414.58 vs MA200=$377.17, buffer=+9.9% — above emergency floor ($376.64 pre-committed threshold); SLV=$63.52 vs MA200=$52.33, buffer=+21.4% — above floor; TSM=$316.50 vs MA200=$287.03, buffer=+10.3% — above floor. All four equity EPS acceleration trajectories confirmed via qualitative refresh today: XOM Q1-2026 EPS=$1.68 ramping to $2.22 by Q3 and $2.15 by Q4; CVX Q1=$1.76 ramping to $2.59 by Q2; LMT Q1=$6.72 ramping to $8.45 by Q4; GOOGL Q1=$2.76 growing slowly to $3.21 by Q4 — weakest ramp in portfolio, confirming replacement candidate evaluation mandate at next rebalance. Pre-logging for next rebalance (2026-04-01): (1) GOOGL 3m=-12.70% is the most severe negative-3m equity in portfolio — primary replacement candidate is COST (mom=+7.18%, 3m=+14.27%, above BOTH MAs, dd63=-5.18%, vol20=15.29%, low-beta defensive consumer staples); (2) TSM 1m=-15.27% — size review if MA50 breach persists post-April 2; (3) SLV dd63=-42.45% — sizing elimination or hold at 2% floor evaluation at next rebalance; (4) GLD MA200 buffer at +9.9% — monitor for compression below +5%.

thesis: NON-REBALANCE DAY: Rebalance due = false. Days since last rebalance checkpoint = 6 (minimum cadence = 7 days; next eligible window = 2026-04-01). The 2026-03-25 rebalance repositioned into full RISK-OFF/DEFENSIVE configuration: XOM=22%, CVX=18% (Energy 40%), GLD=22%, SLV=2% (Commodities 24%), KLAC=10%, TSM=10%, GOOGL=6% (Technology 26%), LMT=10% (Defense 10%). This configuration is operating as designed — portfolio weighted avg momentum ~+27% vs SPY=-1.77% = +29pp alpha spread — and must be allowed to season through the April 2 tariff binary event before the next rebalance evaluation.

FUNDAMENTAL CONFIRMATION: Qualitative tool refresh confirms all four equity EPS trajectories intact. XOM Q1-2026 EPS=$1.68 ramping to $2.22 (Q3) and $2.15 (Q4), then $2.43-$2.51 in 2027 — multi-year operating leverage from oil demand stability. CVX Q1-2026 EPS=$1.76 ramping sharply to $2.59 (Q2) and $2.53 (Q3) — Hess acquisition integration driving cost efficiency; 2027 trajectory reaches $3.09 by Q3-2027. LMT Q1-2026 EPS=$6.72 ramping to $7.28 (Q2), $7.46 (Q3), $8.45 (Q4) — NATO rearmament multi-year cycle fully intact with revenue growing from $18.2B to $21.7B by Q4-2026. GOOGL Q1-2026 EPS=$2.76 growing slowly to $3.21 by Q4-2026 — weakest ramp in portfolio; confirmed replacement candidate evaluation mandate at next rebalance.

REGIME ALIGNMENT: Energy (XOM+CVX=40%) is tariff-insulated — domestic oil production and refining is not a direct tariff target, and energy demand destruction scenario requires a recession not yet confirmed. Defense (LMT=10%) benefits from NATO rearmament spending that is accelerating regardless of tariff regime. GLD (22%) retains 12m=+47.03% de-dollarization/inflation-hedge structural bid; 1m correction from elevated levels does not invalidate the thesis. KLAC (10%) represents quality semi-equipment exposure with strongest 12m=+99% in the portfolio — above MA200 with structural EPS ramp intact despite 1m weakness. Acting today incurs 20-30bps round-trip drag with zero incremental alpha contribution and risks disrupting the rebalance cadence going into the highest-priority evaluation window of the cycle.
risks: MACRO: April 2 'Liberation Day' reciprocal tariff announcement is the dominant binary tail risk. A broad escalation scenario (25%+ tariffs on Taiwan semiconductors, ADRs, or broad import categories) could pressure TSM (direct ADR exposure, 1m=-15.27% already pricing risk) and KLAC (China export restriction co-exposure). SPY below MA200 for 5th+ consecutive session — if SPY drops additional -4% to ~$606 within next 5 sessions, mandatory full defensive review activated outside normal cadence. Oil demand destruction from tariff-induced recession pricing could ultimately reverse XOM/CVX momentum leadership, though current energy momentum (+39%/+30%) shows no deterioration.

IDIOSYNCRATIC: GLD MA200 buffer compressed to +9.9% ($414.58 vs $377.17) — pre-committed emergency trim trigger at <5% buffer (~$396.00) is now within a single adverse session's reach if gold gaps down sharply post-tariff announcement. GOOGL 3m=-12.70% persistent deterioration — if GOOGL closes below MA200 ($262.92) before next rebalance, emergency replacement evaluation activated immediately. SLV dd63=-42.45% with vol20=58.89% — at 2% minimum floor but continued deterioration reinforces case for full elimination at next rebalance.

EXECUTION/LIQUIDITY: All 8 holdings are highly liquid large-cap equities or major ETFs — no execution risk on non-action days. TSM ADR faces April 2 tariff binary at existing 10% weight — if TSM gaps down >8% on April 3 open (below ~$291), monitor for MA200 breach ($287.03) as emergency exit trigger. Turnover cost of unnecessary action today: 20-30bps round-trip with zero marginal alpha. No earnings events for any of the 8 holdings in the next 7 days.

NEXT REBALANCE TRIGGERS (~2026-04-01): (1) GOOGL: 3m=-12.70% and 1m=-12.21% — primary replacement candidate is COST (mom=+7.18%, 3m=+14.27%, above BOTH MAs at $996.58 vs MA50=$986.03 and MA200=$946.34, dd63=-5.18%, vol20=15.29%); replacement threshold: COST score must exceed GOOGL score by >10 points on multi-factor model; (2) TSM: if 1m momentum remains below -12% AND MA50 breach persists post-April 2, size from 10%→7% with proceeds to XOM; (3) SLV: at next rebalance evaluate full elimination (0%) vs minimum floor (2%) — elimination proceeds go to CVX or GLD; (4) Portfolio alpha spread vs SPY must remain above +20pp at next rebalance evaluation — currently +29pp.
trade_of_the_day.why_now: Base case (100%): Mandatory non-action per rebalance cadence policy (days since last checkpoint = 6, minimum spacing = 7 days). The 2026-03-25 rebalance was a high-conviction 4-action repositioning into RISK-OFF/DEFENSIVE configuration that continues to perform as intended — +29pp alpha spread vs SPY, 6 of 8 holdings above MA200, EPS acceleration trajectories intact across all core equity positions. Bull case for doing nothing: April 2 tariff announcement could prove milder than feared ('sell the rumor, buy the news'), benefiting the energy-heavy positioning as oil demand fears recede and energy stocks push to new highs; GLD could recover sharply if tariffs are interpreted as inflationary. Bear case for doing nothing: April 2 tariff escalation creates concentrated tail risk for TSM (10% weight, most direct ADR exposure) — but TSM is already pricing significant downside (1m=-15.27%) and remains above MA200 with 10% buffer, providing adequate defensive runway. Expected edge of doing nothing: preserving full compounding of the 2026-03-25 positioning through the April 2 binary event, avoiding 20-30bps round-trip drag, maintaining the cleanest possible entry state for the 2026-04-01 rebalance which will be the highest-conviction action window of the current 7-day cycle.
